note: Mathlib/Data/Set/Defs.lean:229 `protected def union (s₁ s₂ : Set α) : Set α := {a | a ∈ s₁ ∨ a ∈ s₂}` — literally defines the union of two sets, with `∪` notation via `instance : Union (Set α) := ⟨Set.union⟩` on the next line.

Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/VectorField/LieBracket.lean:77 `def mlieBracket (V W : Π (x : M), TangentSpace I x) (x₀ : M) : TangentSpace I x₀` — vector fields on a manifold are modeled as the bare type `Π x, TangentSpace I x` (a section of the tangent bundle); no standalone `VectorField` def exists, but this and Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/VectorField.lean (vector-space case, `V W : E → E`) formalize substantial vector-field operations (pullback, Lie bracket).

Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Defs.lean:81 `def TensorProduct : Type _ := ...` (module `M ⊗[R] N`) — the general bilinear construction underlying tensors; a classical rank-(p,q) tensor on V is the special case built by iterating this (and its dual) on a single V, which Mathlib has no dedicated named type for.

Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/DivergenceTheorem.lean:266 — the Divergence (Gauss) theorem; the divergence operator itself is written inline as `fun x ↦ ∑ i, f' x eᵢ i` in the docstring/statement rather than given a standalone named def, but this theorem is precisely the formal statement about it.
